user_query:
I’m an international student from Russia studying in the U.S., and I’m trying to organize my personal notes and clean up a few message drafts while I’m feeling homesick. Please work entirely within this workspace using the provided files.

Inputs:
- config/config.yaml
- input/notes.jsonl
- input/messages.jsonl

What I need:
1) Build a structured memories index from input/notes.jsonl using category rules and output paths from config/config.yaml.
- Read categories, keyword lists, and output paths from config/config.yaml. Treat keyword matching as case-insensitive substring matches against both the notes’ text and tags.
- If multiple categories match a note, assign the first matching category according to the order in config.categories. If none match, use the category value "Uncategorized" and an empty matched_keywords list.
- Write a CSV to the exact path specified in the config (which currently is output/memory_index.csv) with these columns and one row per note: id, date, title, category, matched_keywords. matched_keywords should be a semicolon-separated list of unique keywords from the assigned category that appear in the note’s text or tags (case-insensitive), in any stable order you choose.
- Also write a JSON summary to the exact path from the config (currently output/tag_summary.json) mapping each category to an object with fields: {"count": <int>, "ids": [<list of note ids in that category>]}. The counts in tag_summary.json must equal the number of rows per category in the CSV.

2) Rewrite the message drafts in input/messages.jsonl into two tones per message, following rewrite.style and rewrite.signatures in config/config.yaml.
- For each message, create two versions (formal and friendly) that follow the tone guidelines and append the correct signature from the config on a new line.
- Improve clarity and brevity, keep key facts and requests, do not invent new factual content.
- You may adjust the subject for clarity. Keep the audience context (e.g., professor vs. roommate) appropriate to the tone.
- Write the outputs to the exact path from the config (currently output/revised_messages.jsonl) as JSON Lines, with one object per version. Each object must include: id (same as input), tone ("formal" or "friendly"), subject (rewritten), body (rewritten with the appropriate signature appended).

Please do not modify the input files. Use only the information provided. Deliverables must be written exactly to the paths specified in the config so they can be easily checked. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
